title: arenaria aggregata subsp. racemosa
intro: Arenaria aggregata subsp. racemosa, also known as Mountain Sandwort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family and is native to the mountainous regions of Western North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as kidney stones, arthritis, and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Mountain Sandwort include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
